title: [Bug]: https://podman.io/ link to documentation should reference latest release
body:
### Issue Description

https://podman.io/ documentation links to latest which is not yet released and thus does not reflect what the user gets after having installed podman. E.g. currently `podman kube play` contains a `--force` flag which is not yet present in the latest release.
Also `latest` is confusing too. I wouldn't expect it to mean "current state of main branch" but "latest release".

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Go to https.podman.io and click on the documentation link.

### Describe the results you received

By default I see documentation to code that has not been officially released yet.

### Describe the results you expected

I expect to see documentation for the latest release by default.

### podman info output

```shell
--
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: we consider latest to follow along the main branch of podman, not the release.  so latest is very much up to date and is what you would find if you built from main.

Is there a readthedocs option to change default when you open https://docs.podman.io, because there is already the stable link: https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/. I think it would make sense to show stable by default.

There should be a versioned docs for this and let the user change/view the version they want:
https://docs.readthedocs.io/en/stable/versions.html
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@electrofloat @Luap99 @baude @biergit Is this still an open issue?
I'm not using podman anymore. I'm really fed up with it. Went back to docker.

I still see the documentation for the not yet released version being the default when visiting https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/ . So yes to me the issue is still open. 
Btw I just checked how Quarkus, another popular Redhat project, handles it and unsurpisingly [the default version is the latest release](https://quarkus.io/guides/). I think I have never seen it handled like Podman does it but I have to admit I never used Gentoo as well ;)

The only ones with readthedocs access are @baude and @TomSweeneyRedHat AFAIK so they would need to change the default version.
I think it is fine to have latest track the main branch but default when visiting docs.podman.io should be stable.
Sorry, I meant when visiting docs.podman.io it redirects to latest per default.
